title: pellionia radicans var. minima
intro:
Pellionia radicans var. minima, also known as the creeping pellionia, is a variety of low-growing, spreading plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its small, heart-shaped leaves and stems that root at the nodes.

Medicinally, Pellionia radicans var. minima has been used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of Asia, where it is valued for its purported anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.

The health benefits of Pellionia radicans var. minima include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects.

In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Pellionia radicans var. minima are typically dried and made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although their effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically evaluated.
